_comment: sovereign-os doc-coverage dashboard (R491, R454+). Visualizes the operator-§1g doc-coverage instrument that audits each module's coverage across the 6-kind documentation ladder. Sourced from sovereign_os_operator_doc_coverage_query_total{verb,kind,result} (Layer B, SDD-016) emitted by scripts/operator/doc-coverage.py. Operator imports into Grafana via Settings → JSON Model. Closes the future doc-coverage-dashboard waiver and registers doc-coverage in MODULE_COVERAGE at 3 surfaces (core/cli/dashboard).

panels.title: kinds verb (count)
panels.description: Enumerates the 6 doc-kinds in the §1g operator-named documentation ladder.

panels.title: scan verb (count)
panels.description: Operator's primary scan — 'which modules are documentation-shorted today?'

panels.title: gaps verb (count)
panels.description: Doc-gap fail-loud — 'which kind is missing on which module?'

panels.title: doc-coverage query rate per verb (5m)
panels.description: Per-verb query rate over time. kinds / modules / scan / coverage / gaps / selfdef — operator's doc-coverage-tooling usage pattern.

panels.title: doc-coverage kind attention (5m)
panels.description: Per-doc-kind query rate. readme / sdd / helptext / metric-inventory / mandate-row / man-page — operator-revealed-preference signal for which doc-axis is most-watched.

panels.title: doc-coverage result distribution
panels.description: ok / unknown-module / issues — operator-§1g result-class breakdown across all verbs.

panels.title: doc-coverage current state (verb × kind × result)
panels.description: Per-(verb, kind, result) histogram — operator-discoverable: 'which doc-kind am I exercising most, and is it clean?'

options.content:
**Operator §1g verbatim** (sacrosanct):

> "We do not minimize anything."

This instrument enforces the §1g doc-surface delivery contract: each operator-named module must have first-class documentation across the 6-kind ladder, no exceptions silently waived. doc-coverage is one of the 4 instruments aggregated by the `compliance` rollup (alongside surface-map / anti-minimization-audit / ux-design-audit).

**6-kind doc ladder (sacrosanct, R454):**

1. `readme` — top-level README.md mention (operator-named entry)
2. `sdd` — dedicated SDD chapter under `docs/sdd/`
3. `helptext` — `sovereign-osctl` cmd_help section (DX surface)
4. `metric-inventory` — `docs/observability/dashboards/README.md` row
5. `mandate-row` — operator-mandate row (E11.Mx or E10.Mx)
6. `man-page` — stub under `docs/man/`

**Threshold:** All 6 kinds — the documentation ladder has NO partial-credit. Each module either appears in every kind, OR carries an explicit operator-rationalized waiver row. A single missing kind is a documentation gap the operator wants closed.

**CLI:**

```
sovereign-osctl doc-coverage kinds                # enumerate 6
sovereign-osctl doc-coverage modules              # list audited modules
sovereign-osctl doc-coverage scan                 # cross-codebase
sovereign-osctl doc-coverage coverage <module>    # per-module matrix
sovereign-osctl doc-coverage gaps [--module M]    # fail-loud gap list
sovereign-osctl doc-coverage selfdef              # cross-repo (selfdef)
```

**Operator-named:** the documentation surfaces are operator-named verbatim — README / SDD chapter / help-text (DX) / metric inventory / mandate row / man page. Renames are NOT allowed; the names ARE the contract.

**Metric:** `sovereign_os_operator_doc_coverage_query_total{verb,kind,result}` (SDD-016 Layer B)

**Source:** R454 shipped the 6-kind instrument + 6 verbs. R491 (R454+) ships THIS dashboard + registers doc-coverage as a first-class module in MODULE_COVERAGE at 3 surfaces (core/cli/dashboard).

**Standing operator rule (sacrosanct):**

> "We do not minimize anything."

title: sovereign-os doc-coverage
